_readme: Source of truth for /compare/weered-vs-[id] pages. Add an entry, redeploy, get a new comparison page + sitemap entry automatically. Voice rules: honest about competitor strengths, specific about Weered's niche, never attack-piece tone.

competitors.name: Discord
competitors.tagline: The default chat platform for online communities — gaming and beyond.
competitors.summary: Discord is the largest and most established community chat platform. For most communities, it's the right default. Weered exists for the specific case where your community is centered on a single game and benefits from verified-via-API tournaments, presence that tracks what your friends are actually playing, and integrations Discord can't host natively.
use_them_if: Your community spans many topics, not just one game
You need a battle-tested platform with mobile parity and millions of bots
Your members are already on Discord and migration friction is the deciding factor
You want general-purpose voice, video, and chat with the broadest ecosystem
use_weered_if: Your community centers on a single game (Destiny 2, League, MTG, EVE, Path of Exile, etc.)
You want tournaments verified through the game's actual API (Bungie PGCR, Riot, etc.) instead of trust-based brackets
You want presence showing what friends are playing across Steam, Xbox, PSN, Twitch — not just Discord status
You want a lobby-first layout (themed hubs) rather than a server/channel structure
You want deep per-game integrations (Bungie API, Riot API, Lichess, GGG OAuth, Scryfall, etc.) without third-party bot setup
competitors.bottom_line: Discord remains the right call for the vast majority of communities. Choose Weered when your community lives inside specific games and the depth of game-integrated features matters more than the breadth of Discord's general-purpose ecosystem.

competitors.name: Guilded
competitors.tagline: Discord-style chat platform with gaming-team management built in.
competitors.summary: Guilded is the closest direct competitor — both platforms target gaming communities with chat, voice, and organizational tools. Guilded leans toward esports team management (schedules, rosters, scrims); Weered leans toward per-game lobbies with deep API integrations and verified tournaments. Different bets on what gaming-community software should be.
use_them_if: You're running an esports team and need scheduling, rosters, and scrim coordination as first-class features
You want a Discord-like structure (servers, channels) with gaming-specific overlays
You're already on Roblox-owned platforms and want the integration
You need calendar and event management for a competitive team specifically
use_weered_if: You want lobbies that are themed per-game (Destiny 2 hub, MTG hub, EVE hub) rather than per-team servers
You want tournaments verified through the game's own API (PGCR-verified Destiny clears, etc.) — not trust-based brackets
You want presence showing what friends play across Steam/Xbox/PSN/Twitch — not just Guilded activity
You want native data integrations (Bungie, Riot, Lichess, GGG, Scryfall, Moxfield) without bot setup
competitors.bottom_line: Guilded is the better fit for organized esports teams that need roster + scrim management. Weered is the better fit for game-centered communities that want lobby-first structure and game-API-verified events.

competitors.name: TeamSpeak
competitors.tagline: Self-hostable low-latency voice platform with a 20-year gaming legacy.
competitors.summary: TeamSpeak is the legacy voice platform many older gaming communities still run. Its strength is rock-solid low-latency voice and self-hosting control. Weered's strength is a modern chat-and-community layer with deep game-API integrations on top of voice — different generational priorities.
use_them_if: You need self-hosted voice you fully control (legal, compliance, or sovereignty reasons)
You want the lowest possible voice latency for competitive play
Your community is already on a TeamSpeak server and stability outweighs new features
You don't want or need a modern chat/forum/presence layer wrapped around voice
use_weered_if: You want voice + chat + forum + presence + game integrations in one platform
You want hosting handled (no server admin work)
You want native integrations with Bungie, Riot, Lichess, Steam Rich Presence, etc.
You're building a community now, not migrating one with 10 years of TeamSpeak inertia
competitors.bottom_line: TeamSpeak remains the right call for self-hosted, voice-only communities prioritizing latency and control. Weered fits modern communities that want voice plus a full community-platform stack — chat, forum, presence, and game integrations — without running servers.

competitors.name: Revolt
competitors.tagline: Open-source Discord-style platform with strong privacy positioning.
competitors.summary: Revolt is the open-source alternative for people who specifically want a Discord-shaped experience without the closed-source ownership. Privacy and open-source are core values. Weered is closed-source and centralized — different value system, with a focus on gaming-specific depth instead of platform openness.
use_them_if: Open source is a hard requirement for your community
You want a familiar Discord-style layout without the closed-source baggage
You may want to self-host eventually
Privacy + decentralization principles matter more than game integrations
use_weered_if: You're building a gaming community and want native game-API integrations (Bungie, Riot, Lichess, GGG, Scryfall) rather than building them yourself
You want verified tournaments tied to the game's actual API
You want cross-platform presence (Steam/Xbox/PSN/Twitch)
Closed-source is acceptable in exchange for built-in features that would otherwise need engineering effort
competitors.bottom_line: Revolt is the right call if open source is non-negotiable. Weered is the right call if you'd rather have game-depth features built in than the freedom to fork the platform.

competitors.name: Element
competitors.tagline: Matrix-based federated chat with end-to-end encryption.
competitors.summary: Element is the flagship Matrix client — federated, end-to-end encrypted, used heavily by privacy-focused communities and some governments. Weered is a centralized, closed-source platform optimized for game-community depth rather than communication-protocol openness. Built for opposite priorities.
use_them_if: End-to-end encryption is a hard requirement
You need federation (different communities running their own servers but still able to talk)
You're privacy-focused and Matrix's protocol openness matters
Your community has technical members who can run a homeserver
use_weered_if: You're a gaming community and want game-API integrations as a built-in feature
You want verified tournaments tied to the game (Bungie API, Riot, etc.)
You want presence showing what friends are playing across consoles and PC
Federation and end-to-end encryption aren't priorities; built-in gaming depth is
competitors.bottom_line: Element is the right call if E2EE and federation matter most. Weered is the right call if you're building a gaming community and want built-in game-data integrations more than communication-protocol openness.

competitors.name: Steam Chat
competitors.tagline: Steam's built-in friends list, voice chat, and group chats.
competitors.summary: Steam Chat is the chat layer baked into Steam — friends list, voice, group chats, all tied to your Steam account. Free, zero-setup, already where your PC gaming friends are. Weered is a separate platform with deeper community-building tools (lobbies, forums, tournaments) that Steam Chat doesn't try to do.
use_them_if: You only need to coordinate small groups of PC gaming friends
Your community is purely Steam-based with no console crossover
Zero-setup matters more than community-building features
You don't need forums, persistent rooms, or tournament brackets
use_weered_if: Your community spans Steam + Xbox + PSN + mobile and you want unified presence across all of them
You want themed lobbies (Destiny 2, EVE, MTG, etc.) rather than just friend groups
You want persistent forum/discussion alongside chat
You want verified tournaments tied to game APIs
You're recruiting strangers into a community, not just chatting with existing friends
competitors.bottom_line: Steam Chat is great for chatting with PC friends you already know. Weered is for building a game-centered community with persistent structure, cross-platform presence, and verified tournaments that Steam Chat doesn't provide.
